description_long: This obelisk is a tall, rectangular monolith with a smooth, slightly reflective gray surface, resembling concrete or stone. One side features a large, irregularly shaped hole that exposes a rough, fractured interior, suggesting damage or decay. The interior appears hollow and uneven, with visible debris and remnants of structural material, possibly indicating vandalism or weathering. The exterior faces are sharply faceted, with clean geometric lines except for the damaged area. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative motifs on the surface. The base is simple and unadorned, blending seamlessly with the rest of the structure.
description: A tall, gray, rectangular obelisk with a large, jagged hole on one side exposing a rough, hollow interior. The exterior is smooth and faceted, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions.
description_view_0: A plain, smooth, gray rectangular face with no visible damage or features.
description_view_1: A faceted side with a large, jagged hole revealing the rough, hollow interior and debris.
description_view_2: A sharply angled view showing the transition from the smooth exterior to the damaged, hollow section.
description_view_3: A close-up of the damaged side, displaying the full extent of the hole and the rough, debris-filled interior.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple rectangular extension of the obelisk, with no visible steps, platforms, or decorative features. It appears to be made of the same material as the rest of the structure.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and unadorned, matching the rectangular geometry of the obelisk with no unique features or decorations.
